findings.evidence: grep: line 167 states `energyIntegrand A b c = energyIntegrand (1 : Matrix n n ℝ) 0 c + energyIntegrand (A - 1) b 0`; nearby APIs use arbitrary shifted mass (`EnergyForm.lean:129`, `CoerciveEnergy.lean:176`) and Lane D needs bounded/Gårding perturbation decompositions.
findings.fix: State the general split first, e.g. `energyIntegrand A b c = energyIntegrand (1 : Matrix n n ℝ) 0 m + energyIntegrand (A - 1) b (c - m)` (or the fully general base triple version), and derive the current `m = c` case if useful.
findings.issue: `energyIntegrand_eq_one_zero_mass_add_perturbation` fixes the model mass to the same `c`, so it cannot express the natural shifted model with a chosen constant/base mass and a residual mass perturbation.

summary: The coefficient-linearity lemmas are at the expected pointwise level, but the shifted-Laplacian perturbation split is too special for the downstream weak-form use it advertises.
